reviews.title: I had worst experience with them
reviews.body: I had worst experience with them. I lost my money because of their fake promise. I lost my money because of their admin errors. Initially they approved my mortgage and after making all arrangements they rejected. They didn’t even gave a proper explanation for that. They don’t even know what they were doing.

reviews.title: Easy to open a new account. Good interest rates and helpful advisors.
reviews.body: Complete antithesis of another Welsh Building Society initial beginning with "M". With Principality it was easy to open and access accounts online. Competitive interest rates too. I've phoned a couple of times and was answered promptly by a helpful advisor. Tried several times to open a regular savers account with "M" and failed miserably. "M" could learn from Principality as to how straightforward it should be!

reviews.title: worst possible banking experience of my…
reviews.body: worst possible banking experience of my life. deposited money each month, never been able to access it. spoke to CS, sent a reset code in the post, security answers were changed over and over online and yet not one correct answer was ever accepted. ring to highlight my issues with the account and the lady simply said we'll close the account for you then. zero customer service skills and a building society that clearly doesn't care the they haven't got a clue how to manage anyone's money.

reviews.title: All the staff are most efficient
reviews.body: All the staff are most efficient, special thanks to Haley and Holly for outstanding customer service, Having dealt with Building Societies for over 50 Years, Prinipality is without doubt the Market Leader for customer service and both Hayley and Holly are a credit to Principality.I will always invest with them and can highly recommend the company to all and sundry

reviews.title: Caerphilly branch always so nice and…
reviews.body: Caerphilly branch always so nice and polite.Just opened a Christmas regular saver and all of your staff are so kind and friendly.Service is quick please thank themAl

reviews.title: Something positive
reviews.body: I have been taking advantage of the interest rates on the regular savings accounts for a few years now.All on line, never needed to contact anyone.I made a mistake yesterday with a reference number yesterday. Money returned the same day and no doubt I will receive a letter soon explaining why.Interesting to note that there's only one "person", Julie, replying to Trustpilot reviews. If it's the same Julie that's the manager of the Bridgend office then no wonder she's difficult to deal with.I'm very surprised at all the negative reviews.I agree that a banking app would be an improvement.

reviews.title: An excellent facility
reviews.body: An excellent interest rate for the six-month regular saver account and a very prompt and helpful response when I inquired what would happen at maturity. It was easier to speak with someone to give maturity instruction than online. On maturity, my nominated account was credited quicker than expected, and when I inquired about receiving a statement, I was advised that a response would be received within two days. However, an email response provided the document within a few hours of my request.

reviews.title: Wouldn’t allow me to access my child’s…
reviews.body: Wouldn’t allow me to access my child’s savings account to give maturity instructions. Apparently I failed security as I did not know the precise balance - how would I when it’s a postal account and I don’t live anywhere near a branch?

reviews.title: Excellent customer service
reviews.body: Every time I ring PBS with regards to my mortgage I’ve always found the staff friendly , helpful and efficient  They all seem to know their products as well and explain any aspect you are unsure of10 out of 10 from me Excellent customer service

reviews.title: Vague and misleading
reviews.body: I saved £200 each month for 6 months. That is total £1200 and expecting to receive the return based on 7.36% interest.But only received £18... thats it.The information on their website is incomplete, vague and misleading.I compare the details with FirstDirect, and FirstDirect correctly displayed the return that one can get after saving their money for a regular period.Avoid Principality!!!!

reviews.title: Great for beginner savers, clear products, slightly fiddly account referencing
reviews.body: I’ve really enjoyed using Principality’s savings products, especially their 6-month regular savers. As someone new to saving, I’ve found their approach very beginner-friendly, clear terms, helpful guidance, and a genuine sense that they want to support people starting their savings journey.Once a standing order is set up, the process runs smoothly and doesn’t really require my input, which makes it easy to stay consistent. Their regular saver has been a great way to build the habit, and I appreciate how accessible their products are.The only small challenge I’ve found is with account referencing: instead of direct account numbers, you use reference codes to identify your accounts. If you’ve opened more than one, it can get a bit confusing to keep track.Overall, I feel they’re aligned with savers like me, thoughtful, supportive, and easy to engage with. Just a little more clarity on account identification would make the experience even smoother.

reviews.title: Mobile app seriously required
reviews.body: No mobile app for easy access to my account

reviews.title: Thank you Gemma
reviews.body: Never thought i would leave a review for a mortgage company. Spoke to Gemma today who took the time to help me through some difficulties i was having. She went above and beyond showing a level of compassion, patience and understanding i have not experienced speaking to a stranger on the phone. Shes a credit to principality and wanted to just say thank you, so thank you very much for your time today Gemma.

reviews.title: Helpful customer service
reviews.body: I've had my mortgage with Principality for over 4 years. Spoke with Vikki today, I needed a proof of mortgage for my property (I had lost my last statement). She suggested a transaction history and redemption statement, and then emailed it to me whilst on the phone and waited for me to confirm that I'd received it. All staff are incredibly helpful whenever I've called.

reviews.title: feedback removed
reviews.body: feedback removed

reviews.title: Principality are GREAT!
reviews.body: I've always found Principality staff extremely helpful. I phone them once a month when I can't remember which savings account to pay into and they explain it patiently again, every time. Their savings account interest rates are the best I've seen anywhere.

reviews.title: I have had an horrendous experience…
reviews.body: I have had an horrendous experience with the Bridgend branch.  the branch manager Julie,has a serious attitude problem, is extremely rude and patronising.I sold my home and bought a renovation project of a chapel.Julie the branch manager did NOT believe me. My solicitor transferred a large amount of funds from my sale into my account with Bridgend principality,to be used for the renovation of the chapel. I called in On Sat 30th Sept to put notice for £20.000 cash and it was fine.I called in On momday 2nd Sept,and had a bad experience with Julie  she did not want to give me my money,I called in with my daughter on the tues 3rd Sept,and we had a heated conversation as she would not release my funds, she was very obnoxious and rude, and told me to close my account and she would give me a cheque.On Wed !morning I called in again , still no money. I closed my account. I am inthe process of filing an official complaint against Julie , and seeking compensation.My daughter witnessed the whole fiasco and is making a statement. AVOID BRIDGEND PRINCIPALIITY. I am hoping that there is a camera  that filmed this . She will be in trouble. Do not trust this person,she has a hidden agenda.

reviews.title: Staff Are So Helpful, Friendly and Knowledgeable
reviews.body: I had a query re: transferring my ISA to another product within PrincipalityThe 'phone was answered very quickly and LIsa was so helpful and friendly and resolved my query quickly.I've always found that Principalty give consistent and excellent customer service

reviews.title: What a joke first I get cut off 4 times…
reviews.body: What a joke first I get cut off 4 times then once I do get in contact I get told I need to call off of my phone which is at home and I explain I’m calling from my buissness phone then the man on the phone starts telling me that I can’t be the person that is named in the account cause I sound too young ?? What a joke of a company may as well be running a circus

reviews.title: Only one thing needs to change for me
reviews.body: Only one thing needs to change for me; Security. They need to move away from text login access as this is totally vulnerable to sim swap fraud. They need either a biometric app and or authenticator with biometrics like ms or Google or something similar that isn't vulnerable to sim swap.Savings review: Whole process of account set up and maturity is slick and smooth with auto product renewal being an outstanding option. Funds don't go missing for a day or two like some other societies. Even weekend maturities have been faultless. Rates have been market leading with quite a few options too. Hope this continues.

reviews.title: Cold call from a company acting for the PBS
reviews.body: Today I had a cold call from a company called Ronin International. They said they were acting on behalf of the PBS. They wanted to ask me questions for a survey and some of the questions would be of a personal nature. I am disappointed that the PBS would ask this company to undertake cold calling of their members.

reviews.title: Call Handlers are lovely but....
reviews.body: Call Handlers are lovely, but this Society is all about paying ever higher salaries to the Directors...certainly not for providing service to customers. They lower interest rate paid and interpret their "rules" in biased way for their own interests, not members. Best to seek alternatives.

reviews.title: Excellent in every respect
reviews.body: I'm absolutely bewildered by some of the negative reviews that have been appearing on Trustpilot. My experiences of the Principality have been quite the opposite. From my university days decades ago until the present day, their management of customers' affairs and competence has been brilliant. Their website is clearer and easier to use than any other bank/BS website. Staff answer the phone promptly and are knowledgeable and polite. Savings interest rates are very competitive. what distinguishes the Principality from most other financial institutions is that customer experience is great across all generations - I'm proof of that, having first invested with them a very long time ago.

reviews.title: Really disappointing
reviews.body: Up to this year I had been a PBS customer for some 40 years. Staff in branches have always been good but I moved a significant ISA amount this year due to a poor interest rate in comparison to competitors e.g. 2.9% to over 4 on my new product. I did however open a 1 year fixed rate ISA and tried to pay further funds into it this week to receive a terse email from the savings team saying they were returning the additional monies as the product was now closed. I checked when opening the account if I could pay further monies in during the course of the tax year and was told I could. Incompetence and poor customer service in this instance - won't be looking to invest with them any longer. Really disappointed in what was once a good company to deal with.

reviews.title: Poor customer service at times
reviews.body: Poor customer service at times. Some staff don't seem to appreciate customers and find it difficult to give a sincere apology. I shall transfer my account which is a shame as the interest rate is ok. Remember - the Financial Ombudsman may be able to help you if you receive a poor service.

reviews.title: Problem with online banking
reviews.body: I have had numerous accounts with Principality over the years and no problem until recently. However, for several weeks now I have been having problems logging in as I am not receiving passcodes. I have never experienced this problem with any other bank or building society and though I reported the problem some weeks back things have not improved and I am becoming increasingly exasperated. As I do not live locally this is proving a major inconvenience.

reviews.title: Had a problem with a bank transfer…
reviews.body: Had a problem with a bank transfer ,spoke to Claire in the Monmouth branch and was really impressed with her assistance .She sorted everything out for me ,was really helpful and kept me fully informed .Cant praise her enough ,a real asset to the company

reviews.title: Completed Mortgage
reviews.body: Been with this company 14 years.When I have had to contact them, rarely, as it ran smoothly, I have had excellent Customer Service, I last spoke to Sabina, she was more than helpful.Thank you

reviews.title: Really poor website an online service
reviews.body: Really poor website an online service. tried to open an ISA, keep going round in circles. Even though Ive had the letter confirming the acct was open I couldnt access it online. I will now look elsewehere.

reviews.title: KEEP WELL AWAY GO TO HODGE BANK
reviews.body: Absolute shambles of a company well behind competitors,not in todays world,working in the 60s with their systems.They don't even answer the phone what chance have potential customers got.JUST CLOSED MY NEW ACCOUNT

reviews.title: I received excellent customer service…
reviews.body: I received excellent customer service today with a person called RenateAs well as being polite and knowledgeable she went the extra mile to sort out my query with an ISAIt's people like Renata that make the difference in moving a building society from being good to excellentPrincipality building society as well as being the largest in Wales is definitely the bestI'd recommend this building society to anybody

reviews.title: Excellent Experience In Opening A Savings Account
reviews.body: I have opened many on-line savings  accounts with banks and building societies over the years and the Principality BS proved to be the simplest, quickest and best communicated I have experienced. I am pleased I ignored the negative reviews.

reviews.title: Take so long time to complete because…
reviews.body: Take so long time to complete because you ve to go with the system it is joke we are doing with them they are too slow .........I sent them third time mortgage deed by post because they don't like witness signs every time do this or do that :-(

reviews.title: Staff are incapable of using a phone…
reviews.body: Staff are incapable of using a phone and/or cannot afford working phones. Attempting a phone call and they struggle to hear you claiming it is your phone. Strange that when I have plenty of signal and point out is it obviously a fault their end and then they can suddenly understand that accusation.Communication is not their strong point as they sent out maturity instructions with incorrect account information which is why I had to call in the first place to ensure I was receiving the money.

reviews.title: Awfull service
reviews.body: My mortgage was ending within a month. Principality is the institution that I have mortgaged for a long time. I called  and asking a fix rate and deduct it from my year. The interest rate given was 4.70, the highest interest rate in the market. They said that they could not change my mortgage rate even though the interest rates are currently decreasing. I haven't received my contract yet but I dont understand why they cannot reduce it the rate.It was clear that they did not want to help me at all.I definitely do not recommend anyone to get a mortgage from here. I most likely to switch to another mortgage company as soon as possible.

reviews.title: I regularly visit Caernarfon and…
reviews.body: I regularly visit Caernarfon and Pwllheli branches. The staff are fantastic. They're savings are great and they tell you if there is a better rate to be had. I have 3 accounts and an ISA with them. Interest rates are good as well. And they're open every day.

reviews.title: Somewhat painful experience
reviews.body: My wife and I are trying to open Cash ISAs. Their website and procedures are an absolute pain. I eventually managed to open my account to find that, in order to transfer funds from another provider, I had to print out a form then fill it in and post it - in 2025! Haven't they heard of digital signatures? After I'd spent an hour doing mine my wife tried to do hers. The website allowed her to enter my mobile number in error and then told her that she couldn't do this but gave no option to change it. We tried to call them but of course they close at 5. Not impressed.Update: My wife called to update her mobile number and received very prompt and helpful service so upgraded from 1 to 3 stars.

reviews.title: Helpful
reviews.body: Resolved the issue I had the next day

reviews.title: Website a nightmare to use
reviews.body: Website a nightmare to use, locks account after only 3 attempts! Tried to call them to unlock-they are CLOSED FOR TRAINING!? Who does that in 2025! Their authentication process is beyond painful also.

reviews.title: Awful in hospital Major treatment…
reviews.body: Classed as Vulnerable disabled treated Awful in hospital Major treatment needed some help Matthew took 2 weeks to tell me he could not help - just awful - I would not recommend anyone to this company and by the looks of it I'm not on my own feeding information back to the CEO through linekinAlso contacted the BSA as Principality are not following the Mortgage Charter and looking at complaint against customers with Dissability01.02.2025Thank you Julie Principality have now put this right after emailing  Joshua17/02/25Julie can you help me I'm having same problem again I'm in a d out of hospital and need to correspond by email but just received email to say I need to phone - this is very stressful at a time when you don't need it.

reviews.title: Highly recommend (PBS) Principality Building Society.
reviews.body: I cannot understand how or why others have had bad experiences with PBS.I have been with PBS for 2 years and had 3 Savings Accounts that have matured - no problem with the Savings and Interest being paid to my nominated account on the date I requested.Have since opened 4 more Savings accounts with Fixed interest rates of 5.15%, 5.5%, 7% and 8% which although limited by Maximum Amounts are much better that anywhere else.Also have a Flexible Cash ISA with PBS, which I can move money from and back to with no problem and very quickly.

reviews.title: Yet again fabulous service
reviews.body: Yet again fabulous service from Principality. Opened a new ISA and wanted to transfer internally into it, called up the phone was answered really quickly and dealt with by Sarah who openly admitted she wasn't sure how to process the transaction but agreed to find out and ring me back. She did exactly that and my account was funded there and then. Service was friendly and completed with ease.I really don't understand why there's such a disparity in opinion as both Sarah and Molly who dealt with me last time were both exceptional. I also don't have any issues getting into my account online, the process is far less onerous than some other building societies I have accounts with.Keep up the good work 👍

reviews.title: They answer the phone quicker than…
reviews.body: They answer the phone quicker than most. They answer  secure messages normally within 24 hrs. Their savings rates are up in the top 5  on most accounts. In the branch very friendly but not very helpful on internet accounts if there is a problem. Website was terrible , slow sometimes couldn't get a OTPC, so unable to withdraw money. I think that has improved now.

reviews.title: Never answer the phone
reviews.body: Never answer the phone.  The call times are so long.  I am leaving Principality and its not like they are willing to offer you any kind of incentive to stay.

reviews.title: Avoid like the plague! Withdrew mortgage offer at 11th hour!
reviews.body: Mortgage offer withdrawn days before current deal expires, leaving me scrambling for a new (worse) deal. No reason given and Principality won't discuss with me, my broker, or my solicitors.Principality have had my deal agreed for over a month and none of my circumstances have changed. To withdraw with days left on the current term is disgraceful and I've found out that I'm not the only one that's experienced this problem.Avoid this society unless you want to risk the rug pulled from beneath you.

reviews.title: Inaccessible account
reviews.body: Opened an online account early January . Received activation code but couldn’t enter as password not accepted. Managed to change to a new password and reached the activation page - the code was not accepted . Phoned customer services - really helpful but told me a new code had been issued for some reason - I had not requested a new code - puzzling . I was told to request a new code but no facility to do so on the page . Doesn’t inspire confidence .

reviews.title: Top Customer Service
reviews.body: I have to say, Principality Building Society have amongst the most friendly and helpful customer services of any organisation (financial or other) that I've dealt with. They also answer the phone without delays, or the dreaded 'we're experiencing a higher than usual number of calls', your call is important to us' etc..... (like we believe that - what you're really saying is we don't employ enough staff - tough titty)!Both my wife and I have several savings accounts (and have had different ones in the past) with Principality, all of which have been easy to set up and manage, and where we have had a question etc. (only a couple of times), they were quickly, efficiently, and pleasantly answered.

reviews.title: Onion
reviews.body: Yes . Online account can't never registered. It's a scam? They want to go to branch to register at lowest interest rate?  I spent 2 hours in my local branch to register online account with fixed ISA . Still couldn't do it ?

reviews.title: first time buyer
reviews.body: Stay away, T E R R I B L E

reviews.title: I live in scotland
reviews.body: I live in scotland and have a few pbs accounts and every mess up has been me rushing .. every time ive had to call pbs ive explained im deaf and what the problem is AND they have been superb with me. My problem now is of 7th jan 2025 i try to log in does ok till passcode. It says sent your pass code to ...........  which is my number BUT by the time the pass code comes through my 5 minutes to use it is up.if i try again im locked out of my account.. is it pbs or phone networks i dont know but other texts are not running late.. i still give pbs 5 stars because out of all the bank and savings accounts i have these folks have been brilliant with me.nothing is too much trouble they listen to me explain every thing simply so i can relax knowing thats one thing sorted.. five stars from me. Cant fault them

reviews.title: Awful dangerous company.
reviews.body: Awful system.I just remortgage with this company. Poor communication, you have to wait 10 min before somebody answer the phone call after 10 min taking to adviser who doesn’t know what’s going on and telling you he will be call you back but newer calling you back. Very poor solicitor cooperating with Principality. Poor communication and making a lot of mistakes.Conclusion: If you have another choice - used. Don’t take Principality.

reviews.title: Awful system, almost non-existent customer service, DONT BOTHER
reviews.body: JUST READING THROUGH OTHER REVIEWS AND IT SEEMS LOTS OF PEOPLE ARE SUFFERING THE SAME ISSUES!! One reviewer mentioned how wonderful their app is - clearly a "paid" review as they don't have an app. Why would a company be paying for reviews?????Absolutely dire! Opened a savings account with them a few months ago, they don't have an app so you have to login via their website (get with the times - whoever it responsible for customer and IT systems, needs sacking!).So I play the game, register to access my account - within days they are telling me my password is incorrect. I know my password, thank you. Can't login with it, get account blocked. Call them, on the rare occasion they're open, they don't bother to answer the phoneA fee weeks pass by, eventually get through to someone, relatively helpful (I imagine I would be given how few hours they have their phones on for). She resets my account so I can access it again.I try to reset the password, "can't use the same password as that previously used"... So I have to select a different one. What this means is the password saved in my browser/made a not of, was the correct password, but the system wasn't letting me use it. ORGANISATIONS DO THIS WHEN THEY HAVE BEEN HACKED.So I create my new password, thinking it's just a blip, off I go. At the time, it asks me to create some stupid security questions, I mean "first concert" assuming everyone has been to a concert just because they sponsor the millennium stadium - grow up. So I create the answers as requested, and think nothing of it.Eventually receive a code through via mail to access my account, so go to login - password incorrect AGAIN! Only try twice because I know it's going to lock my account and I'll never be able to get hold of them via telephone to unlock it.Do a password reset whilst my account is still active - they send me the OTP via text which is the usual security measure for most banks - no not good enough, now have to answer the stupid security questions, ALL of them, not just one or two. So I pop the answers in, pretty straightforward, and hey presto account locked.So they have £600 of my money and I am not unable to access it until they bother to answer the phones.Ah this point, I don't give a damn about interest, they can shove it wherever feels good for them, I just want my money out and to be gone. Uncontactable over the festive period, not looking forward to trying to get a hold of them again.If you like customer services and service that are modern and actually work, then DONT USE PRINCIPALITY. Additionally, the password constantly being changed indicates they have likely been subject to cyber attacks and unable to mitigate it, or worse they HAVE BEEN HACKED AND LEAKED PERSONAL DETAILS INCLUDING PASSWORDS. That's the only reason why companies mass reset batches of password.

reviews.title: So far so good
reviews.body: So far so good.  Good interest rates.  When I have called, they have answered quickly and were polite and knowledgeable. They have processed forms I sent them quickly. The Website seems fine and you get an opportunity to give feedback each time, and it feels like they are trying to respond to customer feedback. So far no complaints and I am quite impressed

reviews.title: Maturity instructions not honoured and expected to start all over again
reviews.body: My mother's fixed one year savings account is due to mature and her instructions were sent off first class post in plenty of time.  However interest rate changed in that small time window which has never occurred before so her instructions weren't honoured.  Instead of starting again with the form which will be delayed in the Christmas post, she should have automatically been given the slightly lower interest alternative to prevent her funds going into an instant access account which I presume they will now as the next form will be received just before New Year

reviews.title: Great way to save. Easy to use online app.
reviews.body: I opened a regular saver account online a couple of years ago which matured November 2023. A really good interest rate with it, so a nice lump sum in time for Christmas. I was given the option to reinvest a small amount and start the process again when it had matured which I did. I decided to do the same again this year now that this year's one has matured. I also took out a 6 month saver bond which matures on Christmas day and have chosen the option to reinvest a small amount and continue to save for another 6 months. The interest rates on both accounts are very good.I do it all online. It was very easy to set up. I would highly recommend. I can't find a better interest rate anywhere else on these sort of accounts.

reviews.title: Principality in Wales stole my daughters money!
reviews.body: My daughter opened an ISA. She sent in her ID and all was accepted. They transfered her existing ISA and then denied her access to the money. Its not even showing on her new ISA account and its no longer in the previous providers account. Principality are now demanding more evidence that she lives at the address but she doesnt have bills or anything in her name as she has always lived with me. She is absolutely distraught because as a single mum myself I saved and her grandparents saved to get her a nest egg for a deposit for her first home. She is a single mum with a baby and is living with me to save more funds so nothing is in her name and they wont let her access to the money and they wont send it back. Im at a loss and going t have to pay to get some legal advice. Stay away from them - they are absolutely corrupt

reviews.title: Terrible - no stars!
reviews.body: Terrible.  Avoid at all costs - Shrewsbury Branch - I travelled quite a distance to be told I couldn't transfer my money into an account - and why hadn't I filled in a form - told off - I need to make an appointment and yet they had sent me a letter telling me to reinvest my money.  I asked for a cheque for my money and went to invest with West Brom.  I have a few accounts with Principality they seem to forget they work for the customer and I for one will be looking elsewhere.

reviews.title: So far so good...
reviews.body: So far so good...Account opened easily enough, and got set up for a regular payment  - through the website rather than having to copy details to my banking app.It would be nice to have an app too, as I keep all my banking in one group, but not a game changer.

reviews.title: Great Interest Rates
reviews.body: Opened three accounts online in the last two weeks. Everything was straightforward with no problems. I did try opening on line back in October but for some reason site kept saying error towards end of application. Have also telephoned a couple of times with some queries and got excellent service. Great interest rates.

reviews.title: Poor Website
reviews.body: I am an existing customer. I have tried to open an ISA on the website several times over recent days,  but when I get to close to the end of the application, the website keeps saying something has gone wrong.  Made contact with call centre,  but I either have to open it by post ( so now have to wait for a postal pack )  or try again on website. Interestingly the advisor on the call centre, said they hadn't had any complaints about opening accounts online, however, have read one on one of these reviews.Tried again 4 times tonight to open account on website , trying on two seperate devices, keep getting the same message, once I have logged into my account. Rubbish.

reviews.title: So far, so good
reviews.body: I have recently opened 3 Regular Saver accounts paying interest of 8%, 7%(Christmas 2025 Saver account) and the one paying 5.15% p.a. The interest on the 1st one is fixed for 6 months and the last 2 are fixed for 1 year. All the accounts are online and the opening process was straightforward, though, waiting for OTP every time you have to log in to the system is little antiquated. But still no fuss about it.Today I opened another variable interest paying account. Here I need to nominate a bank for me to withdraw the money if I need to before it matures. Now I note that they were unable to authorise this Santander account and asking for bank statement, which is fine, but I couldn't provide this thru online account it seems and I need to call someone on this as I live miles and miles away from Wales. Not sure how this communication will go and worried after seeing the reviews here. I am not yet clear why they are unable to authorise Santander as my linked account when all the funds into the 4 accounts with them have been funded from the same Santander account. This is really yet again antiquated thing.

reviews.title: Is this the 1980's
reviews.body: I wanted to transfer an isa, just realised you guess haven't got an app set up?And by looking at the reviews I don't think I want to now.

reviews.title: Outraged with these again!!
reviews.body: Outraged with these again!!

reviews.title: Unsatisfactory technology
reviews.body: Opened an account in May.  Went online yesterday and the system told me I had no investments with them.  This obviously got me worried but as it was the weekend I could not call anyone.  It seems they had some sort of IT meltdown as later that evening I could not even log in. I tried again this morning and my money is showing up again.  Having read other reviews, it seems they are not very good with modern technology.Also, they have no app, which is much, much more convenient than a website anyway as it allows you to use biometrics to access your account.   However, as they can't successfully organise an error-free website, (based on my experience and reading other reviews), I doubt they could master an app.

reviews.title: Well worth doing with competitive interest rates!
reviews.body: After reading several reviews I was feeling a little put off, but I thought I would try as the Cash ISA rate is very competitive.I registered online, taking note you have 10 mins to complete. Done without a problem. The one time pass code arrived in the post 2 days later to enable me to activate my online account. Also done without a problem.The transfer process was very quick, no problems and my account is now funded. All completed in 5 days.Very happy, no technical online issues, very fast and efficient. My husband has now done the same and no problem there either.

reviews.title: Poor Website But Helpful Staff
reviews.body: I have been trying for several weeks to open an Online ISA account but the website would not recognise me as an existing customer.I spoke to the very helpful Morgan who was patient and talked me through the process (needed to enter my address manually).Poor website but confidence restored with the great help from Morgan.Please sort out the website and pass on my thanks to Morgan who is a credit to your company

reviews.title: LEGALISED LOAN SHARKS
reviews.body: DO NOT take a loan from these people they do not let you pay early without so many penalties that it is just not financially viable. We could have paid them back 6 years ago but they still want all the interest for the full loan.  They are nothing but legalised loan sharks. GO ANYWHERE ELSE GIVE THESE PEOPLE A VERY WIDE BERTH.

reviews.title: Is it me their contact telephone number…
reviews.body: Is it me their contact telephone number cuts one off or if you are lucky enough to get connected it goes around in never ending circles between mortgages and savings Help.....

reviews.title: Branch staff gave me advice on handling…
reviews.body: Branch staff gave me advice on handling my account enabling me to use my maximum tax allowance for the rest of the tax year.

reviews.title: Worse Bank Ever !!
reviews.body: The worse bank ever, been saving here for over 2 decades . Went in branch to draw a little amount of money and they refused to give it to me unless I showed them prof of where I was spending it (ie ) an invoice of work I was having done.I am still speechless.Incompetent staff, with very bad attitudes.

reviews.title: Pathetic
reviews.body: Activated my "account" but that was where the good experience ends!A pathetic website where you cannot open an account. You go round and round in circles with the passcode activation.  Staff helpline are clueless and did not know there was a Xmas account paying 7% where you pay in £125/month...so decided not to bother for £56 return at the end of the year and go somewhere else....Avoid in my opinion as look of the website looks like a scammers site!

reviews.title: No app, old tech which glitches and doesn't work
reviews.body: Martin Lewis recommended a 7% return on some odd looking “Christmas” account so I tried to get one.Could not loginAccount then was frozenThey unlocked the accountTried again would not openTried answering secret questions would not openTried againTried againIt just went into a loop asking the same thingBizarre experience, if they cannot get the tech right then I do not trust them even with one pence.I would take a lesser rate than trust some tin pot bs I have never heard off that cannot invest in decent IT or an app.I am annoyed I wasted my time here.

reviews.title: Saving with Principality
reviews.body: I've had many Savings accounts with Principality over the last 15 years, I live in England miles away from the nearest branch. Everything is easy to transact online, often Principality has better deals than its competitors. I've never had any problems, and whenever I've had to call everyone is very friendly and it seems they go out of their way to help.

reviews.title: A very happy customer
reviews.body: My recent experience is no different than my previous experiences. I have never had a problem using their online service. It's easy to navigate, set up new accounts and transfer money. And I am 84 years old!By the way I see that Lloyds bank is on 1.6 stars, Barclays 1.6 and NatWest 1.4.Principality on 2.9! How good is that?I have also been to their Gorseinon branch who were very friendly and helpful.I'm not naïve, nobody is perfect but they are as good as it gets in my experience.

reviews.title: One of the best mortgage companies out…
reviews.body: One of the best mortgage companies out thereHave my residential and btl with them never had any issues!

reviews.title: Very bad web site..I cannot login due…
reviews.body: Very bad web site..I cannot login due to it being so slow. The login process prompts to send OTP and that arrives before page refreshed to where you can enter the OTP ! Then when you do enter it it actually times out before you login to your accounts. This is after increasing profits from 40 to 60M and making 100 people redundant with their CEO stating this is being done to increase support to their customers !I mean you would think with profits being in the green they could spend some money on their IT, or perhaps invest in producing a mobile app and coming into the 21st century.

reviews.title: Just opened two regular saver accounts
reviews.body: Just opened two regular saver accounts. Straightforward but very cumbersome. Even though I have an account had to apply offline, then log in to see the account. Then, to open the second account had to apply offline again and then log in the see the account. Sadly very cumbersome.Surely it wouldn't take much to enable online account opening. And don't get me going about ISA transfers still being by cheque

reviews.title: Not someone I'm going to trust with my money
reviews.body: Created an account online.  After a few days recieved an activation code by post, except it failed to work, then failed again.  If they can't even get something this simple to work then they really shouldn't be trusted with my money!

reviews.title: Good Service
reviews.body: As a family, we’ve always been satisfied with the service provided at Ebbw Vale. Staff always pleasant and helpful. Good advice, decent rates of interest.

reviews.title: I've never had a problem with this…
reviews.body: I've never had a problem with this Building society. They have good savings rates and every account I've opened it's been plain sailing. I'm a great believer in supporting mutual societies. Customer service I will give more then 5 stars.
